content.problemStatement: Identify and correct the error in the following code
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N (unused), and the second line contains a non‑empty string S consisting only of English letters (a‑z, A‑Z).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of letters in the given string S.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The string "Number" has 6 letters (case does not matter).
First test case: "number" → 6. Second test case: "Number" → 6.

solution.approach: The problem only asks for the length of the given string S. N is irrelevant. For each test case we read and discard N, then read S and output S.length(). This runs in O(total number of characters) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) overall, O(|S|) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) besides the input string storage.
